sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: GoodData joins agentic AI development mix with Agent Builder
feed.summary: GoodData has launched Agent Builder, a new framework designed to simplify the development and management of agentic AI applications. This move positions GoodData alongside other major data management and analytics vendors in the rapidly growing agentic AI space. The Agent Builder leverages GoodData's existing semantic and context management layers to enable the creation of trustworthy and context-aware AI tools, particularly beneficial for ISVs and enterprises seeking to embed analytics without rebuilding agents for every scenario.

feed.title: Ann Arbor Public Power initiative accuses DTE of fronting opposition effort
feed.summary: The Ann Arbor Public Power initiative is accusing DTE Energy of creating a "faux grassroots group" to oppose the city's effort to transition to a publicly-owned electric utility. Campaign filings for the opposition group, the Ann Arbor Responsible Energy Coalition, initially listed DTE's headquarters as its address and a DTE director's email as its contact. DTE acknowledges handling the setup but states the coalition operates independently, citing a study that municipalization would incur significant costs and higher energy bills for Ann Arbor residents, a claim the Ann Arbor Public Power initiative disputes as inflated.

feed.title: Truist cuts HCA Healthcare stock price target on weaker volumes
feed.summary: Truist Securities has lowered its price target for HCA Healthcare Inc (NYSE:HCA) to $535 from $546 due to weaker performance in Q1 2026, primarily from reduced respiratory volumes and adverse weather, which slightly missed estimates. Despite the cut, Truist maintained a Buy rating, citing HCA's financial flexibility and expansion pipeline. Other firms like Stephens, KeyBanc, TD Cowen, and RBC Capital also adjusted price targets downwards but kept positive ratings, while InvestingPro analysis suggests the stock is undervalued with "GREAT" financial health.

feed.title: SolarEdge Technologies Jumps 5.5% After Citigroup Maintains Sell
feed.summary: SolarEdge Technologies (SEDG) saw its stock jump 5.5% after Citigroup raised its price target from $29 to $30, despite maintaining a "Sell" rating. This modest price target increase sparked investor interest in the $2.9 billion market cap company, with analysts still foreseeing potential downside. Investors are advised to watch for further analyst revisions and company updates.

feed.title: TTM Technologies Drops 5.3% Amid Sector-Wide Selling
feed.summary: TTM Technologies experienced a 5.3% drop to $141.05 on Monday, as a broad selloff impacted the electronic components sector. The decline, which saw $14.6 billion in market capitalization shed, was mirrored by peers like SANM and IPGP, indicating sector-wide concerns rather than company-specific issues. Investors are advised to monitor whether this sector weakness continues and to watch for further industry commentary on demand or supply chain dynamics.

feed.title: Coca-Cola’s Q1 2026 Preview: Pricing Power and Tariff Exposure Test the Defensive Rally
feed.summary: Coca-Cola is set to report its Q1 2026 earnings, which will be a key test of whether its recent stock performance is driven by strong execution or merely a flight-to-safety by investors. Analysts expect approximately $12.244 billion in net revenue and $0.81 non-GAAP comparable EPS, with a focus on whether the company can maintain organic revenue growth amidst new aluminum tariffs, currency weakness, and commodity inflation. Investors will pay close attention to the split between price/mix and volume contribution to organic growth, as sustained reliance on pricing alone could signal demand elasticity risks.

feed.title: Thermo Fisher Scientific Signs Agreement to Sell its Microbiology Business to Astorg
feed.summary: Thermo Fisher Scientific Inc. has announced a definitive agreement to sell its microbiology business to Astorg, a pan-European private equity firm, for approximately $1.075 billion. The microbiology business, which generated $645 million in revenue in 2025, provides antimicrobial susceptibility testing and culture media solutions. The transaction is expected to close in the second half of 2026, subject to customary conditions and regulatory approvals, and is anticipated to be slightly dilutive to adjusted earnings per share in the first full year.

feed.title: Deere & Co agrees to pay $99 million to settle ‘right to repair’ lawsuit
feed.summary: Deere & Co. has agreed to pay $99 million to settle a class-action lawsuit alleging the company monopolized repair services for its agricultural equipment. The settlement, which still requires court approval, aims to resolve accusations that Deere withheld repair software and colluded with dealers, forcing farmers to use authorized services at higher prices. Despite denying wrongdoing, Deere agreed to the settlement to move forward, while still facing separate litigation from the Federal Trade Commission regarding its repair practices.

feed.title: United Airlines (UAL) and American Airlines (AAL) Merger Talks Collapse
feed.summary: United Airlines CEO Scott Kirby confirmed he approached American Airlines about a potential merger, which American Airlines CEO Robert Isom rejected, calling it "anticompetitive." The Trump administration also opposed the merger. Both airlines have cut their full-year profit guidance due to factors like higher jet fuel prices, with UAL stock down 17% year-to-date and AAL down over 20%.

feed.title: Keurig faces another class action over allegedly misleading ‘recyclable’ K-Cup claims
feed.summary: Keurig Dr Pepper is facing a new class action lawsuit alleging that it falsely advertises its K-Cup pods as recyclable. The plaintiff, Tracy Sulli, claims that most recycling centers cannot process K-Cups due to their small size, irregular shape, multi-material construction, and contamination issues. This lawsuit follows a similar settlement in 2023 regarding Keurig's recycling claims, with the plaintiff seeking damages and injunctive relief for New York consumers.

feed.title: Erste Group Initiates RTX Corp(RTX.US) With Hold Rating
feed.summary: Erste Group has initiated coverage on RTX Corp (RTX.US) with a Hold rating. The report provides an overview of RTX Corp and its market position.

feed.title: S&P Global faces earnings test as bond issuance rebounds
feed.summary: S&P Global is set to report its first-quarter earnings, with analysts anticipating a rebound driven by increased bond issuance, expecting earnings of $4.82 per share on $4.08 billion in revenue. Investors are closely watching the performance of the company's Ratings and Indices segments, as well as its operating margin expansion, to assess if its premium valuation is justified by recovering debt issuance and growing index volumes.

feed.title: Chico's FAS Unveils First-Ever Credit Card Program and Reimagined Loyalty Experience Across Chico's, Soma, and White House Black Market in partnership with Synchrony and Mastercard
feed.summary: Chico's FAS has partnered with Synchrony and Mastercard to launch its first-ever co-branded and private label credit card programs, alongside reimagined loyalty programs for its brands: Chico's, White House Black Market (WHBM), and Soma. These new initiatives aim to provide customers with enhanced rewards, expanded payment options, and a more streamlined and engaging loyalty experience. The programs offer various benefits, including higher rewards on purchases, discounts, and exclusive offers, leveraging Synchrony's digital capabilities and Mastercard's global network.

feed.title: Displaced by luxury housing developer Toll Brothers, Chatham Estates residents demand compensation
feed.summary: Over 140 families in Cary, North Carolina, face displacement from Chatham Estates, their mobile home community, by June 30 as luxury developer Toll Brothers plans to build new homes on the land. Despite state law requiring notice, there is no mandate for compensation or relocation assistance, leaving residents with difficult and costly choices like selling at low prices or sharing housing. Residents are directly pressuring Toll Brothers, seeking $2 million for relocation, while a town-created $800,000 fund is deemed insufficient to cover moving expenses that can reach tens of thousands of dollars.

feed.title: Brown & Brown earnings on deck as property rate pressures test
feed.summary: Brown & Brown Inc. is set to release its first-quarter earnings, with analysts anticipating EPS of $1.36 and revenue of $1.89 billion. Investors will be closely watching for signs that commercial property insurance rate declines are stabilizing and for updates on the integration of the Accession Risk Management Group acquisition. The stock has been under pressure due to slowing organic revenue growth and ongoing competitive threats.

feed.title: Norfolk Southern Analysts Boost Their Forecasts Following Upbeat Q1 Earnings
feed.summary: Norfolk Southern (NYSE: NSC) reported better-than-expected Q1 earnings, with adjusted diluted EPS surpassing estimates despite a slight decline from the prior year. Following these results, Evercore ISI Group and BMO Capital analysts raised their price targets for the company. President and CEO Mark George noted the company's focus on operational discipline amidst various challenges.

feed.title: Ecolab set to report earnings as $4.75B data center bet looms
feed.summary: Ecolab is preparing to report Q1 earnings, with analysts expecting adjusted EPS of $1.70 and revenue of $4.03 billion. Investors will focus on the company's organic growth and the impact of its $4.75 billion investment in AI data center cooling through its acquisition of CoolIT Systems. The market is also keen on management's comments regarding pricing power, margin expansion, and the integration of recent acquisitions like Ovivo Electronics.

feed.title: Williams-Sonoma Launches Dormify to Help Decorate Cramped College Quarters
feed.summary: Williams-Sonoma has officially launched Dormify as its 10th brand, less than a year after acquiring its intellectual property. Dormify targets students and young consumers, offering products for compact living spaces, inspirational content, and design guidance. The brand also introduced a "Pick-Up Near Campus" program, leveraging existing Williams-Sonoma stores for convenient order collection.

feed.title: TD Bank (NYSE: TD) issues $2.0M bail-inable callable notes due 2029
feed.summary: The Toronto-Dominion Bank priced $2.0 million in callable fixed-rate notes due April 28, 2029, with an annual interest rate of 4.25%. These notes are bail-inable, meaning they can be converted into common shares under Canadian bank resolution powers, carrying inherent risks for investors. The offering proceeds to TD were $1,986,000 after underwriting discounts, and the notes are not listed on any securities exchange.

feed.title: PACCAR earnings in focus as truck orders show signs of revival By Investing.com
feed.summary: PACCAR Inc. is set to announce its first-quarter earnings, with analysts anticipating declines in EPS and revenue compared to the previous year, though sequentially improved from Q4. Investors are closely watching for signs of sustained truck order momentum and the impact of PACCAR's decision to eliminate tariff surcharges on its gross margins and market share. The company's Parts and Financial Services segments are crucial for stable earnings amidst challenging new truck sales, as management forecasts a potential rebound in U.S. and Canadian Class 8 truck sales for 2026.

feed.title: Humana partners with Mark Cuban's Cost Plus Drugs on technology, distribution
feed.summary: Humana announced a pharmacy partnership with Mark Cuban's Cost Plus Drugs, where Humana's CenterWell pharmacy unit will act as a distributor and use Cost Plus Drugs' SwiftyRx digital platform. This collaboration aims to enhance operational efficiency, reduce costs, and allow Humana to offer mail-order prescriptions to qualifying employees, also intending to develop new structures for employer-sponsored health plans. Cost Plus Drugs operates on a fixed-markup model for medications, promoting affordable access.

feed.title: Keurig Dr Pepper reports Q1 2026
feed.summary: Keurig Dr Pepper reported a solid start to 2026 with increased net sales in Q1, reaching $4.0 billion, up 9.4%. CEO Tim Cofer highlighted strong momentum in cold beverages and coffee results meeting expectations, alongside the successful acquisition of JDE Peet's. The company reaffirmed its full-year guidance for 2026, expecting net sales of $25.9-$26.4 billion and low-double-digit constant currency Adjusted diluted EPS growth.

feed.title: Morgan Stanley cuts ResMed stock price target on market competition By Investing.com
feed.summary: Morgan Stanley has reduced its price target for ResMed (NYSE:RMD) to $286 from $310, while maintaining an Overweight rating due to anticipated market competition, particularly from Philips re-entering the

feed.title: BMO raises Norfolk Southern stock price target to $310 on merger potential
feed.summary: BMO Capital has increased its price target for Norfolk Southern Corp. (NYSE: NSC) to $310 from $305, maintaining a Market Perform rating, despite the stock currently trading higher at $318.50. The raised target is primarily based on the potential regulatory approval of a merger with Union Pacific, which BMO uses to anchor its valuation. Although Norfolk Southern's Q1 2026 results exceeded expectations, BMO noted that the company's operating performance lags peers, and InvestingPro suggests the stock is overvalued relative to its fair value.

feed.title: BlackRock vs. Schwab: One Financial Dividend Is Built to Last a Generation
feed.summary: This article compares the dividend durability of BlackRock (BLK) and Charles Schwab (SCHW), both of which raised their dividends after their Q1 earnings reports. BlackRock's dividend strength is attributed to its consistent fee-compounding asset management model and long history of uninterrupted growth, even through the 2008 financial crisis. In contrast, Schwab's dividend, while accelerating, is more sensitive to interest rate environments and has a less consistent track record, despite its strong Q1 performance driven by net interest revenue.

feed.title: Truist reiterates Hold on Biogen stock ahead of tau therapy data
feed.summary: Truist Securities has reiterated a Hold rating on Biogen Inc. with a $189.00 price target, ahead of anticipated BIIB080 Phase 2 CELIA data expected by mid-year. While the current BIIB080 therapy is unlikely to be commercially viable due to administration challenges, positive data could validate the tau hypothesis in Alzheimer's and accelerate the development of more practical treatments. The stock has seen a 55% return over the past year, and analysts generally maintain a Hold rating, though InvestingPro suggests it is undervalued.

feed.title: NYSE Content Update: Former NASA Head to Ring Closing Bell for NYSE Space Summit
feed.summary: The New York Stock Exchange announced that former NASA Administrator Jim Bridenstine will ring the Closing Bell for the NYSE Space Summit. This event follows a pre-market daily advisory from the NYSE, which noted lower equities on Monday due to rising oil prices and upcoming earnings reports from five Magnificent 7 companies. Bridenstine will also discuss the 75% increase in the Space Force budget.

feed.title: Here’s Why Stevanato Group Spa (STVN) Declined in Q1
feed.summary: Stevanato Group S.p.A. (NYSE:STVN) experienced a decline in Q1 2026, as noted by Conestoga Capital Advisors in their investor letter. The company's stock struggled due to investors weighing solid results against a mixed underlying business mix and moderating growth profile, with overall revenue growth remaining relatively modest at 5%. Despite improved margins and scaling high-value solutions, the stock was pressured by weakness in the Engineering segment, a transition towards higher-value products, and the decline of oral vs. GLP-1s.

feed.title: Verizon Stock In Rally-Mode After Beat-and-Raise
feed.summary: Verizon Communications Inc (NYSE: VZ) stock is up 3.8% to $48.10 after reporting adjusted first-quarter earnings of $1.28, beating estimates. The company also raised its full-year profit forecast and is discussing AI infrastructure integration with hyperscalers. Despite a recent drawdown, VZ is testing a key trendline, and a shift in analyst sentiment could help it reach its March peak again.

feed.title: These Analysts Slash Their Forecasts On HCA Healthcare After Q1 Results
feed.summary: Following its Q1 results, HCA Healthcare Inc. (NYSE: HCA) saw several analysts reduce their price targets despite reporting in-line earnings and increasing revenues. The hospital operator affirmed its 2026 earnings guidance, causing its shares to rise by 2.2%. Analysts from Truist Securities, Oppenheimer, Keybanc, and Stephens & Co. all maintained their positive ratings but lowered their respective price targets.

feed.title: Deutsche Bank Adjusts eBay Price Target to $132 From $118, Maintains Buy Rating
feed.summary: Deutsche Bank has increased its price target for eBay to $132, up from $118, while reiterating a Buy rating on the stock. This adjustment comes amidst other analyst actions on eBay, including BofA Securities raising their target to $110 and Cantor Fitzgerald maintaining a Neutral rating with a target of $100. The article also mentions recent insider share sales and regulatory comments regarding eBay's anticipated acquisition of Depop Limited.

feed.title: Xylem faces earnings test with analysts trimming expectations By Investing.com
feed.summary: Xylem Inc. is set to report its first-quarter earnings, facing scrutiny as analysts trim expectations and Wall Street views its valuation as stretched despite long-term sector promise. Investors will be watching for revenue growth acceleration, margin performance, and management's stance on M&A and capital allocation. The report will test whether recent estimate cuts reflect a recalibration or the start of a deeper reassessment of the company's near-term trajectory amidst a growing IoT water management market.

feed.title: CNBC: Jon Gray on Blackstone’s Q1 2026 Results
feed.summary: Blackstone's Jon Gray discussed the company's Q1 2026 financial performance in an interview with CNBC. The article directs readers to the full Q1 2026 results for more details. It also provides various quick links and contact information for Blackstone's divisions and press inquiries.

feed.title: Costco has plans for a second NH location at the former Fox Run Mall in Newington
feed.summary: Costco Wholesale Club is planning to open its second New Hampshire location at the former Fox Run Mall in Newington, named Seacoast Landing. This would be the first prospective tenant for the redeveloped mall space, with Costco filing paperwork for review and a preliminary consultation scheduled for April 27. Costco previously had a Newington location and currently only operates a wholesale warehouse in Nashua.

feed.title: Procter & Gamble Analysts Raise Their Forecasts Following Strong Q3 Earnings
feed.summary: Procter & Gamble (NYSE: PG) delivered strong third-quarter financial results, surpassing analyst expectations for both earnings per share and sales. Following this positive performance, analysts have adjusted their price targets for the company, with one firm raising its target while another slightly lowered its target but maintained an "In-Line" rating. The company also affirmed its full-year adjusted EPS and sales guidance.

feed.title: Insider sale: Prashanth Mahendra‑Rajah (NYSE: UBER) sold 18,000 shares
feed.summary: Prashanth Mahendra‑Rajah, associated with Uber Technologies (NYSE: UBER), has filed a Form 144 notice indicating proposed sales of Common Stock. This filing details two sales of 9,000 shares each on April 16, 2026, and April 23, 2026, totaling 18,000 shares. The document also lists several scheduled stock bonus issuances for Mahendra‑Rajah throughout 2025.

feed.title: Incyte earnings on deck: Povorcitinib prospects meet Q1 reality
feed.summary: Incyte is set to report its first-quarter earnings, with analysts anticipating increased revenue and EPS year-over-year but a sequential decline, partly due to seasonal headwinds affecting core products like Opzelura and Jakafi. Investors will be closely watching the company's progress with povorcitinib, its oral JAK1 inhibitor, which has shown significant efficacy in trials for hidradenitis suppurativa and is seen as a potential blockbuster to offset the looming patent cliff for ruxolitinib. The report will test Incyte's ability to manage current challenges while advancing its pipeline for future growth.

feed.title: Navy's MQ-25A makes first flight with Lockheed ground controls
feed.summary: Lockheed Martin's Skunk Works MDCX platform successfully controlled the first flight of the U.S. Navy's Boeing MQ-25A Stingray on April 22, 2026, from MidAmerica St. Louis Airport. The MDCX software, selected for the MQ-25A in 2020, functions as the ground control component of the Navy's UMCS MD-5 ground control station, showcasing a ready-now, open-architecture command and control capability for carrier-based unmanned operations. This milestone is crucial for the Navy's Air Wing of the Future vision.

feed.title: Capitalizing on Towers: Crown Castle’s Q1 2026 Restructuring and Path to Improved Liquidity
feed.summary: Crown Castle (CCI) reported its Q1 2026 financial results, showing a significant recovery in net income due to a decrease in losses associated with the sale of its Fiber Business. While top-line site rental revenues and Adjusted EBITDA decreased, the company is undergoing a strategic transition to a pure-play U.S. tower business, selling its Fiber segment for $8.5 billion. Management expects to use the sale proceeds to repay debt and repurchase shares, maintaining its full-year 2026 outlook despite anticipated headwinds from tenant cancellations.

feed.title: Shareholders urge Meta (NASDAQ: META) to publish class-level vote tallies
feed.summary: Shareholders are urging Meta Platforms (NASDAQ: META) to adopt Proposal #6, which requests the company disclose voting results separately by share class starting after its 2027 annual meeting. The proposal, filed as an exempt solicitation, aims to enhance transparency for holders of one-vote Class A shares, arguing that the current aggregated tallies obscure potential divergences from the ten-vote Class B shares predominantly held by Mark Zuckerberg. Proponents highlight a structural imbalance where Zuckerberg's ~14% economic interest grants him ~61% voting power, and suggest that class-level reporting would provide clearer insights into shareholder support for governance matters.

feed.title: Digital Realty cut to Hold at HSBC as strong AFFO growth already priced in (DLR:NYSE)
feed.summary: HSBC downgraded Digital Realty Trust (DLR) to Hold from Buy, causing its stock to slip by 1.6% in Monday morning trading. Analyst Phani Kanumuri believes the strong AFFO per share growth is already priced into the stock, making sustained growth unlikely. Investors are advised to consider this revised outlook.

feed.title: She left Citigroup after 18 years as one of its top women. Why Ida Liu chose HSBC as her next move
feed.summary: Ida Liu, former top woman at Citigroup, has joined HSBC as CEO of its private bank, managing $566 billion in assets. She chose HSBC due to its strong alignment with Asian wealth management, particularly in Hong Kong, and her Chinese background. Liu also discussed the "glass ceiling" and "bamboo ceiling" challenges for women and Asian leaders in the corporate world, emphasizing the need for more female and Asian representation in CEO roles.

feed.title: RIMS Names Prologis’ Jeff Bray as 2026 Risk Manager of the Year
feed.summary: RIMS has announced Jeff Bray of Prologis as the 2026 Risk Manager of the Year, recognizing his leadership in integrating risk management into global business strategy. Emily Buckley of Specialized Bicycle Components was inducted into the Risk Management Honor Roll for building a modern risk function from the ground up. These awards highlight their innovative approaches to risk, including enterprise-wide programs and cost-optimization strategies, and their commitment to advancing the risk management profession.

feed.title: Iron Mountain (IRM) Valuation In Focus After Recent Share Price Strength And Undervaluation Debate
feed.summary: Iron Mountain (IRM) is under scrutiny after its share price rose to $116.03, showing strong momentum over the past month and five years. The company is considered undervalued, trading about 6.6% below analyst targets and at an estimated 27% discount to its intrinsic value of $160.00, driven by its stable physical storage business and growing digital and data center operations. Investors are encouraged to assess the underlying financials and risks associated with the bullish fair value narrative.

feed.title: Iron Mountain (IRM) Valuation In Focus After Recent Share Price Strength And Undervaluation Debate
feed.summary: Iron Mountain (IRM) has seen significant share price strength recently, climbing 18.04% over the last 30 days and 253.92% over five years. Despite this, analysts suggest the stock, currently trading at $116.03, is undervalued by approximately 27% with a fair value of $160.00. This valuation is based on its stable physical storage business and growing digital solutions and data center operations.

feed.title: Sandisk edges up ahead of Q3 earnings; Morgan Stanley increases estimates, target
feed.summary: Sandisk (SNDK) shares edged up by 5% ahead of its Q3 fiscal 2026 earnings release, scheduled for April 30. Morgan Stanley has increased its price target and EPS estimates for the company prior to the announcement.

feed.title: Hasbro expects March cyberattack to impact second-quarter revenue
feed.summary: Hasbro announced that a cyberattack in March is expected to impact its second-quarter revenue and operating profit due to delays in order processing and shipping. While systems are being restored and most delays are anticipated to be recovered in the second half of the year, the company will incur investigation costs. Hasbro also reported strong first-quarter preliminary results, exceeding analyst expectations, and reiterated its full-year guidance, leading to a positive outlook from Bank of America analysts.

feed.title: Nio launches aftermarket assisted driving indicator light upgrade for Chinese customers
feed.summary: Nio has introduced an aftermarket assisted driving indicator light upgrade service for Chinese customers, effective April 27, 2026, to enhance the experience of existing vehicles. This upgrade, costing 998 yuan ($146) with a 10 yuan deposit discount, features a dual-color light design for turn signals and a blue light for seven assisted driving scenarios. It is compatible with several older Nio models, bringing a feature that is becoming standard on newly launched EVs.

feed.title: U.S. Stocks: Dow Inc. Shows Little Movement, Down 4 Dollars; Nasdaq Also Down (Morning of the 27th).
feed.summary: The Dow Inc. stock experienced little movement, showing a slight decrease of $4. The Nasdaq also saw a decline during the morning trading session on the 27th.

feed.title: TCOM Investors Have Opportunity to Lead Trip.com Group Limited Securities Fraud Lawsuit with the Schall Law Firm
feed.summary: The Schall Law Firm has announced a class action lawsuit against Trip.com Group Limited (NASDAQ: TCOM) for securities fraud, encouraging investors who purchased securities between April 30, 2024, and January 13, 2026, to join before May 11, 2026. The lawsuit alleges that Trip.com made false and misleading statements by downplaying regulatory risks related to monopolistic business practices, causing investors to suffer losses.
